name: FlashBlock
description: Low-latency Solana transaction acceleration service with SwQoS-enabled direct connections, staking-prioritized peer-to-validator access, global private fiber network with dynamic leader-aware routing, and binary protocol to minimize serialization overhead. Reduces latency by ~500ms vs standard RPC nodes and cuts processed TX receipt overhead by 40-50ms. Also supports BSC (BNB Smart Chain) bundle submission.

api_key.note: Most endpoints require an API key in the request header, except tip-floor and error-details endpoints.
api_key.how_to_get: Visit https://www.flashblock.trade/ to register and log in. Create and retrieve your key within the console.

tip_wallet_note: Randomly select an address from the list for your transfer, which helps distribute the load and improves system efficiency.
tip_note: 0.0001 SOL minimum. Recommended: 0.0001-0.01 SOL (adjusted based on congestion). High priority: 0.01+ SOL for urgent transactions. Tip floor is dynamically calculated based on network conditions.
cost_on_failure: Tip is a SOL transfer instruction within your transaction. If the transaction fails/reverts, the tip is not paid (same as Jito model). Transactions array must include at least one transaction to a tip address, otherwise validation fails (error 1011).

tip_strategy.adaptive: Poll tip-floor endpoint periodically. Increase tip by ~10% if P95 confirmation latency exceeds threshold or consecutive failures rise. Decrease slowly (~-5% every 5 min) when metrics recover.

tip_floor.note: Returns recommended tip amount in SOL based on current network conditions. Dynamically calculated, query periodically to adapt.

solana.note: Use HTTP2 long connections (Keep-Alive) for best performance. Keep-Alive max duration is 30 seconds; send a GET request to root endpoint every ~30s to maintain connection.

rate_limits.default: HTTP 429 returned when rate limit exceeded
rate_limits.error_code_429: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded
rate_limits.note: Specific Solana rate limits not publicly documented. Error codes 429/1006 indicate limiting; back off and retry.

submit_batch_rest.description: REST API batch transaction submission. Primary recommended method.

response_success.message: The transaction was submitted successfully, but the status of the transaction is pending.

submit_batch_rest.curl_example: curl -X POST "https://ny.flashblock.trade/api/v2/submit-batch" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Your API Key" -d '{"transactions": ["base64_encoded_transaction_1"]}'

sendTransaction_jsonrpc.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 compatible sendTransaction for use with existing Solana client libraries.
sendTransaction_jsonrpc.curl_example: curl -X POST "https://ny.flashblock.trade/" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Your API Key" -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"sendTransaction","params":[["base64_encoded_tx"],{"encoding":"base64"}]}'

get_tip_floor.description: Returns the recommended tip amount in SOL, dynamically calculated based on current network conditions.

get_transaction_error_details.description: Retrieve error details for a failed transaction by its signature.

message: Transaction failed: Transaction is not recent

bsc_sendBundle.description: Submit a bundle of signed BSC transactions.
bsc_sendBundle.note: Effective gas price for all packets must be >= 0.05 gwei. Priority = weighted avg gas price * 0.9 + BNB transfer to 0xfb888290676ba64063ae7b165dae41511aab36ed

bsc_bundleStatus.description: Check if a BSC bundle has been included on-chain.

encoding.note: Transactions must be correctly encoded in base64 format. Error code 1015 is returned for invalid base64.

swqos.description: SwQoS-enabled direct connections with staking-prioritized peer-to-validator access

private_fiber_network.description: Global private fiber network with dynamic leader-aware routing

binary_protocol.description: Binary protocol engineered to minimize serialization overhead

latency_reduction.description: ~500ms reduction vs standard RPC nodes, 40-50ms reduction in processed TX receipt overhead

automatic_retry.description: Automatic retry mechanisms to mitigate packet loss

keep_alive.description: HTTP Keep-Alive supported. Max duration 30 seconds. Send GET to root endpoint every ~30s to maintain connection. HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 enable keepalive by default.

sandwich_protection.description: Add any valid Solana public key beginning with 'jitodontfront' to any instruction in your transaction. Any bundle including a TX with this account will be rejected unless the TX is first (index 0) in the batch.
sandwich_protection.account_requirements: Does NOT need to exist on-chain, but MUST be a valid public key. Mark as read-only to optimize landing speed.

batch_submission.description: Submit multiple transactions in a single batch request
batch_submission.max_batch_size: Limited (error 1007 if exceeded, error 1010 for transaction count exceeded)

shred_stream.description: Ultra-low latency Solana data streams before blocks finalize. Push-based UDP delivery model.
shred_stream.setup: Log into flashblock.trade/login to configure. Specify receiving IP/port, select region, ensure firewall permits designated UDP port.

referral_program.description: Up to 30% rebates on friend earnings

error_codes.401: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication credentials
error_codes.403: Forbidden - The requested operation is not permitted
error_codes.429: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded
error_codes.1010: Transaction Count Exceeded - split the batch and resubmit
error_codes.1012: Transaction Already - already in the network, do not resubmit
error_codes.1013: This RPC endpoint only allows sendBundle or sendTransaction
error_codes.1015: The requested data is not valid Base64
error_codes.1030: User identification information does not exist

important_notes: Transactions array must include at least one transaction to a tip address, otherwise validation fails
Transactions must be correctly encoded in base64 format
Batch submissions have quantity limits; exceeding results in error 1007
Ensure wallet has sufficient funds to avoid error 1003
Once submitted and confirmed, transactions cannot be reversed
Use HTTP2 long connections for optimal latency
Send keep-alive ping every 30 seconds to maintain connection
